author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XCII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 1. …We are not Christians, except on account of a future life: let no one hope for present blessings, let no one promise himself the happiness of the world, because he is a Christian: but let him use the happiness he hath, as he may, in what manner he may, when he may, as far as he may.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When it is present, let him give thanks for the consolation of God: when it is wanting, let him give thanks to the Divine justice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let him always be grateful, never ungrateful: let him be grateful to his Father, who soothes and caresses him: and grateful to his Father when He chasteneth him with the scourge, and teacheth him: for He ever loveth, whether He caress or threaten: and let him say what ye have heard in the Psalm: “It is a good thing to give thanks unto the Lord; and to sing praises unto Thy Name, Thou Most Highest” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This Psalm is entitled, a Psalm to be sung on the Sabbath day.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lo, this day is the Sabbath, which the Jews at this period observe by a kind of bodily rest, languid and luxurious.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They abstain from labours, and give themselves up to trifles; and though God ordained the Sabbath, they spend it in actions which God forbids.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our rest is from evil works, theirs from good; for it is better to plough than to dance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They abstain from good, but not from trifling, works.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God proclaims to us a Sabbath.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What sort of Sabbath?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: First consider, where it is.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is in the heart, within us; for many are idle with their limbs, while they are disturbed in conscience.…That very joy in the tranquillity of our hope, is our Sabbath.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the subject of praise and of song in this Psalm, how a Christian man is in the Sabbath of his own heart, that is, in the quiet, tranquillity, and serenity of his conscience, undisturbed; hence he tells us here, whence men are wont to be disturbed, and he teaches thee to keep Sabbath in thine own heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 3. …Accuse thyself, and thou receivest indulgence.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Besides, many do not accuse Satan but their fate.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: My fate led me, saith one when you ask him, why did you do it? why did you sin? he replies, by my evil fate.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lest he should say, I did it; he points to God as the source of his sin: with his tongue he blasphemes.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He saith not this indeed openly as yet, but listen, and see that he saith this.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: You ask of him, what is fate: and he replies, evil stars.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: You ask, who made, who appointed the stars; he can only answer, God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It follows, then, that whether he doth so directly or indirectly, still he accuseth God, and when God punisheth sins, he maketh God the author of his own sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It cannot be that God punishes what He hath wrought: He punisheth what thou doest, that He may set free what He hath wrought.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But sometimes, setting aside everything else, they attack God directly: and when they sin, they say, God willed this; if God had not willed it, I should not have sinned.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Does He warn thee for this, that not only He may not be listened to, to keep thee from sin, but even be accused because thou dost sin?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then doth this Psalm teach us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “It is a good thing to confess unto the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is to confess unto the Lord?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In both cases: both in thy sins, because thou hast done them; and in thy good works, confess unto the Lord, because He hath done them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then shalt thou “sing unto the Name of God, the Most Highest:” seeking the glory of God, not thine own; His Name, not thine.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if thou seekest the Name of God, He also seeketh thy name; but if thou hast neglected the Name of God, He also doth blot out thine.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “To tell of Thy mercy early in the morning, and of Thy truth in the night season” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is the meaning of this; that the mercy of God is to be told us in the morning, and in the night the truth of God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The morning is, when it is well with us; the night, the sadness of tribulation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then did he say in brief?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When thou art prosperous, rejoice in God, for it is His mercy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now, perhaps thou wouldest say, If I rejoice in God, when I am prosperous, because it is His mercy; what am I to do when I am in sorrow, in tribulation?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is His mercy, when I am prosperous; is it then His cruelty, when I am in adversity?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If I praise His mercy when it is well with me, am I then to exclaim against His cruelty when it is ill?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No. But when it is well, praise His mercy: when ill, praise His truth: because He scourgeth sins, He is not unjust.…During the night Daniel confessed the truth of God: he said in his prayer, “We have sinned, and committed iniquity, and have done wickedly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O Lord, righteousness belongeth unto Thee: but unto us confusion of face.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He told of the truth of God during the night-season.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is it to tell of the truth of God in the night-season?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not to accuse God, because thou sufferest aught of evil: but to attribute it to thy sins, His correction: to tell of His loving-kindness early in the morning, and of His truth in the night-season.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When thou doest this, thou dost always praise God, always confess to God, and sing unto His Name.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Upon a psaltery of ten strings, with a song, and upon the harp” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye have not heard of the psaltery of ten strings for the first time: it signifies the ten commandments of the Law.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But we must sing upon that psaltery, and not carry it only.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For even the Jews have the Law: but they carry it: they sing not.…“And upon the harp.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This means, in word and deed; “with a song,” in word; “upon the harp,” in work.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou speakest words alone, thou hast, as it were, the song only, and not the harp: if thou workest, and speakest not, thou hast the harp only.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: On this account both speak well and do well, if thou wouldest have the song together with the harp.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Thou, Lord, hast made me glad through Thy works; and I will rejoice in giving praise for the operations of Thy hands” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye see what he saith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast made me living well, Thou hast formed me: if by chance I do aught of good, I will rejoice in the work of Thy hands: as the Apostle saith, “For we are His workmanship, created unto good works.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For unless He formed thee to good works, thou wouldest not know any works but evil.…Because thou canst not have truth from thy own self, it remains that thou drink it thence, whence it floweth: as if thou hast gone back from the light, thou art in darkness: as a stone glows not with its own heat, but either from the sun or fire, and if thou withdraw it from the heat, it cools: there it appears, that the heat was not its own; for it became heated either by the sun or by fire: thus thou also, if thou withdraw from God, wilt become cold; if thou approach God, thou wilt warm: as the Apostle saith “fervent in spirit.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Also what saith he of the light?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou approach Him, thou wilt be in light; therefore saith the Psalm, “Look upon Him, and be lightened; and your faces shall not be ashamed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because therefore thou canst do no good, unless lightened by the light of God, and warmed by the spirit of God; when thou shalt see thyself working well, confess unto God, and say what the Apostle saith; say unto thyself, that thou be not puffed up, “For what hast thou that thou didst not receive?”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That wretched man who doeth good, and suffereth evils, seeth him, becometh disturbed, and saith, O God, the wicked, I imagine, please Thee, and Thou hatest the good, and lovest those who work iniquity.…The Sabbath being now lost in the inner man, and the tranquillity of his heart being shut out, and good thoughts repelled, he now beginneth to imitate him whom he seeth flourishing amid his evil deeds; and turneth himself also to evil works.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But God is long-suffering, because He is eternal, and he knoweth the day of His own judgment, where He weigheth all things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Teaching us this, what saith he?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O Lord, how glorious are Thy works: Thy thoughts are made very deep” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Verily, my brethren, there is no sea so deep as these thoughts of God, who maketh the wicked flourish, and the good suffer: nothing so profound, nothing so deep: therein every unbelieving soul is wrecked, in that depth, in that profundity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost thou wish to cross this depth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Remove not from the wood of Christ’s Cross: thou shalt not sink: hold thyself fast to Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What do I mean by this, hold fast to Christ?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It was for this reason that He chose to suffer on earth Himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye have heard, while the prophet was being read, how He “did not turn away His back from the smiters, and His face from the spittings of men,” how “He turned not His cheek from their hands;” wherefore chose He to suffer all these things, but that He might console the suffering?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He could have raised His flesh at the last day: but then thou wouldest not have had thy ground of hope, since thou hadst not seen Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He deferred not His resurrection, that thou mightest not still be in doubt.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Suffer then tribulation in the world with the same end as that which thou hast observed in Christ: and let not those who do evil, and flourish in this life, move thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thy thoughts are very deep.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where is the thought of God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Rejoice not as the fish who is exulting in his bait: the fisherman hath not drawn his hook: the fish hath as yet the hook in his jaws.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what seemeth to thee long, is short; all these things pass over quickly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is the long life of man to the eternity of God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost thou wish to be of long-suffering?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Consider the eternity of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thou regardest thy few days, and in thy few days thou dost wish all things to be fulfilled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What things?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The condemnation of all the wicked: and the crowning of all the good: dost thou wish these things to be fulfilled in thy days?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God fulfilleth them in His own time.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why dost thou suffer weariness?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is eternal: He waiteth: He is of long-suffering: but thou sayest, I am not of long-suffering, because I am mortal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But thou hast it in thy power to become so: join thy heart to the eternity of God, and with Him thou shalt be eternal.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this reason, after saying, “Thy thoughts are very deep,” he at once subjoins: “An unwise man doth not well consider this, and a fool doth not understand it” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are the things which an unwise man doth not well consider, and which a fool doth not understand?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “When the ungodly are green as the grass.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “as the grass”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They flourish when it is winter, but they will wither in the summer.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou observest the flower of the grass?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What more quickly passeth by?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is brighter?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is greener?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not its verdure delight thee, but fear its withering.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast heard of the ungodly being green as the grass: hear also of the righteous: “For lo.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the mean while, consider the ungodly; they flourish as the grass; but who are they who understand it not?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The foolish and unwise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “When the ungodly are green as the grass, and all men look upon the workers of iniquity” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All who in their heart think not aright of God, look upon the ungodly when they are as green as grass, that is, when they flourish for a time.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why do they look upon them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “That they may be destroyed for ever.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they regard their momentary bloom, they imitate them, and wishing to flourish with them for a time, perish for evermore: this is, “That they may be destroyed for ever.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But Thou, Lord, art the Most Highest for evermore” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Waiting above in Thy eternity until the season of the wicked be past, and that of the just come.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Listen, brethren.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Already he who speaketh (for he speaketh in our person, in the person of Christ’s body, for Christ speaketh in His own body, that is, in His Church), hath joined himself unto the eternity of God: as I a little before was saying unto you, God is long-suffering and patient, and alloweth all those evil deeds which He seeth to be done by wicked men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore? because He is eternal, and seeth what He keepeth for them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost thou also wish to be long-suffering and patient?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Join thyself to the eternity of God: together with Him wait for those things which are beneath thee: for when thy heart shall have cleaved unto the Most Highest, all mortal things will be beneath thee: say then what follows, “For lo, thine enemies shall perish.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those who now flourish, shall afterwards perish.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are the enemies of God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Brethren, perhaps ye think those only enemies of God who blaspheme?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They indeed are so, and those wicked men who neither in tongue nor in thought cease to injure God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what do they do to the eternal, most high God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou strike with thy fist upon a pillar, thou art hurt: and thinkest thou that where thou strikest God with thy blasphemy, thou art not thyself broken? for thou doest nothing to God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the enemies of God are openly blasphemers, and daily they are found hidden.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Beware of such enmities of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Scripture revealeth some such secret enemies of God: that because thou knowest them not in thy heart, thou mayest know in God’s Scriptures, and beware of being found with them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: James saith openly in his Epistle, “Know ye not that the friendship of the world is enmity with God?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast heard.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dost thou wish not to be an enemy of God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be not a friend of this world: for if thou art a friend of this world, thou wilt be an enemy of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For as a wife cannot be an adulteress, unless she be an enemy to her own husband: so a soul which is an adulteress through its love of worldly things, cannot but be an enemy to God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It feareth, but loveth not: it feareth punishment but is not delighted with righteousness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All lovers of the world, therefore, are enemies of God, all the curious after trifles, all consulters of diviners astrologers, and evil spirits.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them enter, or not enter, Churches: they are enemies of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They may flourish for a season like grass, but they will perish, when He beginneth to visit them, and pronounce His sentence upon all flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Join thyself to the Scripture of God, and say with this Psalm, “For lo, thine enemies shall perish” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be not found there, where they shall perish.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And all the workers of iniquity shall be destroyed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 11. …“But mine horn shall be exalted like the horn of an unicorn” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why did He say, “like the horn of an unicorn”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sometimes an unicorn signifies pride, sometimes it means the lifting up of unity; because unity is lifted up, all heresies shall perish with the enemies of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And “mine horn shall be exalted like an unicorn.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When will it be so?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And mine old age shall be in the fatness of mercy.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why did he say, “my old age”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He means, my last days; as our old age is the last season in our lives, so the whole of what the body of Christ at present suffereth in labours, in cares, in watchings, in hunger, in thirst, in stumbling-blocks, in wickednesses, in tribulations, is its youth: its old age, that is, its last days, will be in joy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And beware, beloved, that ye think not death meant also, in that he hath spoken of old age: for man groweth old in the flesh for this reason, that he may die.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The old age of the Church will be white with good works, but it shall not decay through death.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What the head of the old man is, that our works will be.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye see how the head groweth old, and whiteneth, as fast as old age approacheth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou sometimes dost seek in the head of one who groweth old duly in his own course a black hair, yet thou findest it not: thus when our life shall have been such, that the blackness of sins may be sought, and none found, that old age is youthful, is green, and ever will be green.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye have heard of the grass of sinners, hear ye of the old age of the righteous: “My old age shall be in the fathers of mercy.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And Mine eye hath beheld on mine enemies” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whom doth he call his enemies?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All the workers of iniquity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do not observe whether thy friend be wicked: let an occasion come, and then thou provest him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou beginnest to go contrary to his iniquity, and then thou shalt see that when he was flattering thee, he was thy enemy; but thou hadst not yet knocked, not to raise in his heart what was not there, but that what was there might break out.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Mine eye also hath looked upon mine enemies: and mine ear shall hear his desire of the wicked that rise up against me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In my old age.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, in old age?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the last times.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what shall our ear hear?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Standing on the right hand, we shall hear what shall be said to them that are on the left.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The grass withereth, the flower of sinners dieth away: what of the righteous?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The righteous shall flourish like a palm tree” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The ungodly are green as grass; “The righteous shall flourish like a palm tree.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By the palm tree he signifieth height.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Possibly he had also this meaning in the palm, that in its extremities it is beautiful: so that thou mayest trace its beginning from the earth, its end in its topmost branches, wherein its whole beauty dwelleth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The rough root appeareth in the earth, the beautiful foliage toward the sky.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy beauty too, then, shall be in the end.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy root is fixed fast: but our root is upward.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For our root is Christ, who hath ascended into heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Humbled, he shall be exalted; “he shall spread abroad like a cedar in Libanus.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See what trees he spoke of: the righteous shall flourish like a palm-tree: and shall spread abroad like a cedar in Libanus.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When the sun hath gone forth, doth the palm-tree wither?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Doth the cedar die?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But when the sun hath been glowing for some hours, the grass drieth up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The judgment, therefore, shall come, that sinners may wither, and the faithful flourish.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Such as are planted in the house of the Lord, shall flourish in the courts of the house of our God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They shall be yet more increased in fruitful old age, and shall be quiet, that they may show it forth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such is the Sabbath, which but a little while ago I commended unto you, whence the Psalm hath its title.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They shall be quiet, that they may show it forth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore are they quiet that show it forth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The grass of sinners moveth them not: the cedar and palm-tree not even in tempests are bent.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They are therefore quiet, that they may show it forth: and with reason, since at present they must show it forth even unto men who mock at it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O wretched men, who are lovers of the world!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those who are planted in the house of the Lord, show it to you: those who praise the Lord with song and lute, in word and deed, show it forth to you, and tell you.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be not seduced by the prosperity of the wicked, admire not the flower of grass: admire not those who are happy only for a season, but miserable unto eternity.…If ye wish to flourish like a palm-tree, and to spread abroad like a cedar in Libanus, and not to wither like grass when the sun is hot; as those who appear to flourish when the sun is absent.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If then ye wish not to be as grass, but as the palm-tree and the cedar, what will ye show forth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “How true the Lord my strength is: and that there is no unrighteousness in Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How is it there is no unrighteousness?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A man committeth so great crimes; he is well, he hath sons, a plentiful house, he is full of pride, is exalted by his honours, is revenged on his enemies, and doeth every evil deed; another man, innocent, attending to his own affairs, not robbing another’s goods, doing nothing against any one, suffereth in chains, in prison, tosseth and sigheth in poverty.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How is it that there is no unrighteousness in Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be quiet, and thou shalt know: for thou art disturbed, and in thy chamber thou dost darken thy light.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The eternal God doth wish to shine upon thee: do not then make thee cloudy weather from thy own disturbed mind.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be quiet within thyself, and see what I say unto thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because God is eternal, because for the present He spareth the bad, bringing them to repentance: He scourgeth the good, instructing them in the way unto the kingdom of heaven: “There is no unrighteousness in Him:” fear not.…What, if He leaveth this man unpunished now, because he is doomed to hear, “Depart into everlasting fire.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But when? when thou shalt be placed at the right hand, then shall it be said to those placed on the left, “Depart into the everlasting fire, which is prepared for the devil and his angels.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not therefore those things move thee: Be quiet, keep Sabbath, and show “how true the Lord my strength is: and that there is no unrighteousness in Him.”
